item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Give to the ants Thorium-232. What could go wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These ants haven't been near Unseen University, have they?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept of this SCP is awesome. Extremely strong ants? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It IS kind of similar to other stuff, both on this site and off it, but I like it enough to upvote it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote 831 and thought this looked familiar. Haven't read closely yet, so no vote yet.

I read it again, and I think there's room for this and 831. The technical writing is well done here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's pretty much where I was going. The Foundation's thoughts at this time are something like "Let's see where they're going with this". If the ants leave the planet on their own it would certainly solve the short-term issue of keeping them from causing trouble with society, but it raises questions about where they're going, why they want to leave Earth, and what they plan to do when they get there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it because they're distinctly lacking in any malice towards us. They just want to do their thing, and they're pretty cool. Nifty little refresher between scares.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking about 831 as well, and I'll third this sentiment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carrying_capacity

Otherwise not a bad SCP (the ants-building-a-nuclear-reactor thing broke my disbelief too much for an upvote, but otherwise good) but the term "carrying capacity" means something different from how you're using it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So long, and thanks for all the thorium…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read SCP. Thought it was too similar to SCP-831 as people said and prepared to downvote.

Then, I looked at the "launch windows."

██/██/20██, , WILL NOT REOPEN UNTIL 2███.

I got that! Brilliant and boosts the awesome of this SCP into the stratosphere. As Deacon_Blues_117 said, these guys have a specific destination in mind.

AND I THINK I RECOGNIZE IT!

I think these guys are aiming their spacecarft at the asteroid belt, or to be more exact on a course for SCP-1923.

Upvoted and I wish I could do it twice.

EDIT: Also do you mind if I use these little fellows in a Tale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the reference to Phase IV.

I first wanted to question why anyone was even considering giving the super-intelligent ant colony sophisticated materials, but there's just enough mystery to let it slide. Why are the ants launching something? Is there some high-level cooperation going on between the Foundation and the SCP? Is there an agreement for the SCP to leave for other planets as a more economical means of "containment"?

Apparently it's been covered before, but this one gets my vote. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is exactly what I thought upon reading this. This is very well written, though, so I don't feel that it's worthy of a downvote, but it really does feel too much like a crossbreed of those other two SCPs for me to feel comfortable upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's why I like this one; we have enough of the other kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the concept of ants that appear to be preparing to leave the planet - for somewhere very specific, given the launch windows…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BREEDER REACTOR MUST BE ONLINE BY 20██ IN ORDER TO COMPLETE PHASE VI ON SCHEDULE.

Made me laugh out loud. No downvote from me; I think this article has some serious potential despite its similarities to SCP-831

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "in constant movement through a series of tubes built into the device." I see what you did there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concept is too close with other SCPs we have… The first thing I feel about this article is 'wow haven't we don't this before?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the real world, a single ant can carry about 0.2 grams at most. To lift even a single kilogram would require 5,000 ants, which would necessarily limit the colony's ability to construct massive machines. Thus I felt it necessary to give them a strength buff to explain their ability to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this, but

The carrying capacity of SCP-1942 workers is well in excess of typical P. occidentalis capacity; a single worker has been observed to be able to lift and carry objects weighing up to 0.4 kg without injuring itself. Larger groups of SCP-1942 have demonstrated an ability to lift objects weighing several hundred kilograms. The mandibles of SCP-1942 workers are also significantly more powerful than is typical of their species; swarms of SCP-1942 working in unison have demonstrated an ability to cut through stainless steel with a precision impossible to humans without computer-operated tools.

seems unnecessary. being superintelligent ants on a mission is enough, they don't need to also be super strong and impossibly precise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voted and replied before getting to the addendum. Cleared my downvote after reading it but I'm still too on the fence to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would say this is different enough from either of those (which I hadn't read before) to not be a retread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been done.
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-671
http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-831

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've definitely succeeded (in my opinion).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the idea for this article tonight after reading another new insect SCP that was of the same "It crawls inside your body and turns you into a body horror so it can reproduce" vein that keeps popping up. I wanted to prove you could write an article about an anomalous insect that doesn't kill you horribly and doesn't use human bodies for its reproductive cycle, and I think i've succeeded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's well written for sure but I'm a little 'drained' with all the insect/insect-like critters that build or break down complex structures.

No vote for now.

P.S. - Smapti, what ever it is you're doing to churn out these great articles is definitely working.
